name: PyQt6 (Qt6 Python Bindings by Riverbank Computing)
pricing_notes: GPL-3.0 for open source; commercial license via Riverbank Computing for proprietary apps

performance.notes: Native Qt rendering; OpenGL via QOpenGLWidget; hardware-accelerated

agent_readiness.notes: Third-party Qt6 Python bindings by Riverbank Computing. from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QApplication, QWidget, QPushButton. from PyQt6.QtCore import Qt, pyqtSignal. app = QApplication([]). window = QWidget(). button = QPushButton('Click me', window). button.clicked.connect(lambda: print('clicked')). window.show(). app.exec(). Signals/slots: pyqtSignal. PyQt6 vs PySide6: PyQt6 is GPL or commercial; PySide6 is LGPL/official. API is nearly identical to PySide6. QML: via PyQt6.QtQml. Qt Designer .ui: from PyQt6.uic import loadUi.

gotchas: GPL-3.0 requires commercial license for proprietary applications
Nearly identical API to PySide6 — causes confusion about which to use
C++ Qt documentation requires mental mapping to Python
PyQt6 and PySide6 cannot be installed in same environment without conflicts
best_when: When existing PyQt5 codebase is being migrated to Qt6
Teams with PyQt5 expertise moving to Qt6
When specific PyQt6 features (like pyqtSignal type safety) are preferred
avoid_when: Commercial proprietary applications (GPL incompatibility — use PySide6 instead)
New projects with no Qt history (PySide6 is official and LGPL)
When PySide6 already installed in environment
summary: PyQt6 provides Qt6 Python bindings by Riverbank Computing — nearly identical API to PySide6 but GPL-3.0 or commercial (vs PySide6's LGPL). QApplication + QWidget + signals/slots. Full Qt6 widget set, QML, OpenGL. ~100MB install. pyqtSignal for type-safe signals. For new projects prefer PySide6 (official, LGPL) unless migrating from PyQt5 codebase.
